narrative:
Short impact summary

The article titled "Global urban environmental change drives adaptation in white clover," published in Science, has garnered significant scholarly attention, as evidenced by its 189 citations. This research, backed by esteemed institutions such as Uppsala University and Stanford University, identifies critical environmental adaptations in white clover amid urbanization.

Full narrative

The research presented in "Global urban environmental change drives adaptation in white clover" (2022) has made a notable impact in the field of ecology, receiving 189 citations in scholarly literature. Such recognition underscores the significance of this study and highlights the ongoing discourse regarding urban environmental changes and their biological implications.

The collaborative effort from leading institutions, including Uppsala University and Stanford University, speaks to the quality and validity of the research. This institutional backing indicates a robust foundation for exploring how urban environments affect plant adaptability, specifically in the case of white clover. The endorsement from the Natural Environment Research Council further solidifies the research's importance and relevance in the context of environmental science.

While the article has yet to spark extensive online discussions on platforms such as Reddit or Bluesky, its presence on Wikipedia signifies community interest and acknowledgment of its societal relevance. Such mentions play a crucial role in bridging the gap between academic research and public understanding, showcasing the broader impact of the study on ecological awareness.

The article has also influenced subsequent research, as indicated by proven downstream reuse of the findings or materials in 190 cases. This level of reuse demonstrates the technical value of the study and affirms its role as a foundational research output in understanding adaptation strategies in urban settings. Overall, this research is a testament to the critical intersection of urban development and ecological responses, highlighting the importance of continued investigation in this domain.
publisher: American Association for the Advancement of Science
